features.description: Neon schema migrations: port the 8 Supabase SQL migrations to db/migrations/ (drop pg_cron and RLS/anon/authenticated constructs; keep memories table with vector(1536) + HNSW index, system_config, and the three SQL functions), add a psql-based migration runner, and write the Neon provisioning runbook for Ovidiu. ACCEPTANCE: runner applies all migrations to an empty Neon database idempotently (second run is a no-op, exit 0); schema assertion script against a Neon branch confirms expected objects present and no pg_cron/RLS/anon/authenticated grants.
features.coverage: not measurable (bash scripts; vitest v8 coverage does not apply). Behavioral test covers both scripts end-to-end: 16 checks incl. idempotency, schema presence, dropped-construct absence, and both failure paths.
features.notes: Acceptance verified against the real Neon database (ep-curly-glade-ab0ouns8, aws-eu-west-2, PG 18) on 2026-07-03: run 1 = 5 applied, run 2 = no-op exit 0, assert-schema.sh all 16 checks OK. 8 source migrations became 5: RLS (004) and pg_cron/process_pending_memories (005) dropped per AD-3/AD-7, 008 constraint folded into the new 002 (fresh start, identical end state).
approaches_tried: TDD via bash behavioral test against a local pgvector/pg17 Docker container, then acceptance re-run against the provisioned Neon DB. psql runner records each migration in schema_migrations inside the same --single-transaction as the migration file. Worked first pass.

features.description: MCP server DB layer on Neon: replace the supabase-js client factory (mcp-server/src/db/client.ts) with @neondatabase/serverless, rewrite all queries.ts functions to plain SQL, remove @supabase/supabase-js from mcp-server/package.json, add @vitest/coverage-v8 so coverage is measurable. ACCEPTANCE: existing Vitest suite green + new integration round-trip test against a Neon branch (capture_memory -> search_brain finds it -> list_recent lists it -> get_stats counts it); coverage >= 95% on touched code.

Shared Worker DB module at workers/shared/: Neon driver wrapper, env handling, and the common SQL used by the Workers. ACCEPTANCE: module builds standalone; unit tests cover connection creation, query helpers, and a ::vector cast round-trip (insert an embedding array, read it back identically).

Capture Worker: port supabase/functions/capture to a Cloudflare Worker fetch handler with JWT HS256 + HMAC auth preserved verbatim, Neon insert with explicit ::vector cast, and AD-6 degradation (on embedding/metadata failure the memory is stored with embedding_status 'pending'/'failed'). ACCEPTANCE: deployed Worker returns 201 with memory id for validly signed requests (JWT and HMAC paths both tested), 401 for invalid/missing auth, 400 for invalid payload; on forced embedding failure the row lands with embedding_status='pending'.

Retry Worker: port supabase/functions/retry-worker to a Cloudflare Worker scheduled() handler on a '* * * * *' Cron Trigger, no public HTTP route; retry eligibility comes solely from the get_retry_eligible_memories() SQL function (the PostgREST .or() fallback is not ported, per AD-7). ACCEPTANCE: cron-triggered run processes a seeded pending memory to complete within one scheduled invocation; HTTP requests to the Worker return 404/405; overlapping runs do not double-process (status transitions + batch limit).

Remote MCP Worker: stateless Streamable HTTP via the Cloudflare Agents SDK createMcpHandler(), exposing the four existing tools (search_brain, list_recent, get_stats, capture_memory); JWT issuance endpoint (/auth/token) ported; Express SSE host (mcp-server/src/transport/sse.ts) removed. ACCEPTANCE: an MCP client connects over Streamable HTTP to the Worker URL with a JWT from /auth/token and successfully invokes all four tools; unauthenticated connection is rejected.

CLI setup rewrite (personal-use scope; 60-min non-coder test dropped per 2026-07-03 decision): Neon connection step, migration-runner step, wrangler deploy/secrets steps; validate.ts and status.ts off supabase-js; Supabase-specific steps removed. ACCEPTANCE: setup completes end-to-end on a machine with a Neon connection string and authenticated wrangler (writes .env, runs migrations, deploys the three Workers, sets secrets); status command reports all components healthy with no supabase-js dependency.

Cutover + decommission: fresh Neon data (Supabase data explicitly not preserved, decided 2026-07-03), reconfigure Claude Desktop stdio env, point webhook callers at the capture Worker custom domain (workers.dev interim fallback), verify end-to-end, decommission Supabase project lxwtqegyhrfixnfctkne. Downtime unconstrained. ACCEPTANCE: runbook executed — Claude Desktop stdio works against Neon; capture Worker reachable on its domain; retry cron observed processing; Supabase decommissioned only after all other criteria observed green (rollback bound).

Docs + spec amendment PR: amend open-brain-spec.md §1.3 (drop 60-minute non-coder setup test; personal-use), §5 Architecture and §8 Deployment for the Neon + Cloudflare stack; update README; .env.example gains DATABASE_URL and the missing OPENAI_METADATA_API_KEY, loses all SUPABASE_* vars; replace docs/deploy-to-supabase.md. ACCEPTANCE: spec PR merged or approved by Ovidiu; .env.example matches the new stack with no SUPABASE_* vars; README quickstart matches the new stack.
